records.ety: [OE. anoien, anuien, OF. anoier, anuier, F. ennuyer, fr. OF. anoi, anui, enui, annoyance, vexation, F. ennui. See Annoy, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Annoyed (n*noid"); p. pr. & vb. n. Annoying.]

defs: To molest, incommode, or harm; as, to annoy an army by impeding its march, or by a cannonade.

records.ety: [OE. anoi, anui, OF. anoi, anui, enui, fr. L. in odio hatred (esse alicui in odio, Cic.). See Ennui, Odium, Noisome, Noy.]

defs: A feeling of discomfort or vexation caused by what one dislikes; also, whatever causes such a feeling; as, to work annoy.
quotes: Worse than Tantalus' is her annoy.
